title: 顺水推舟
summary:
Hercule Poirot questions the motives of the Claude family, suggesting that their sheltered upbringing left them ill-equipped to handle a loss of security and that members of the family may have harbored murderous intentions toward Rosaline Claude. Rolly Claude admits that he once considered killing Rosaline during a visit to his farm, noting he could have staged it as an accident, though he did not act on the thought.

Poirot reconstructs the actual crime, which Rolly confesses was a killing committed in a rage. Rolly explains that after visiting his uncle Jeremy, he discovered a photograph revealing that Jeremy and Francis had attempted to defraud one of Rosaline's relatives. Enraged by this betrayal, Rolly returned to the Stagg Inn and confronted a man in room five. Rolly punched the man in the jaw, causing him to fall and hit his head on a marble hearth, resulting in instant death. Upon inspecting the body, Rolly discovered a lighter with the initials "D.H.," identifying the deceased as David Hunter. To cover his tracks, Rolly moved the body, cleaned the scene, and tampered with the man's watch to set the time to 9:10.

Rolly further admits to manipulating the investigation. He had found a man who intended to provide false testimony regarding the deceased's identity, but after finding the man had committed suicide, Rolly destroyed a note intended for the coroner that would have clarified the man was not Robert André Devereaux. Rolly confesses his motives were to obtain money to bribe Lynn and to ensure Hunter was convicted, though he expresses confusion as to why the case against Hunter was ultimately dropped.
